metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_3032
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.2: "Laminins are a component of CNS synapses"
- "Four different antibodies detect the laminin β2 chain in our preparations of hippocampal synaptosomes: C1, 5F11, D79, and C4 (Fig. 2b). Each of these antibodies also detects the β2 chain in a matrix prepared from Müller cells, which are known to produce the β2 chain in the retina (Libby et al., 1997) (e.g., C4, Fig. 2b). Thus, we conclude that the β2 chain is likely a component of laminins in the extracellular matrix at the hippocampal synapse."

Fig.3+Table 1: "Hippocampal synapses lacking the laminin β2 chain are malformed"
- "We calculated the width of the synaptic cleft by measuring the distance between the pre- and the post-synaptic membrane at 4 different positions (see diagram in Table 1) along the synapse: at each edge of the active zone, as defined by the edges of the post-synaptic density (PSD) (A and D), and near the center of the active zone (B and C). In β2 KO mice, the average distance between the pre- and post-synaptic membrane is 31% greater than in wild-type mice (Table 1). The width of the synaptic cleft in the β2 KO mice is also highly inconsistent, presenting an unusual variation along the length of the synapse in the distance between the pre- and post-synaptic membranes (compare width at positions A, B, C, and D in Table 1). In contrast, there is minimal variation in the wild-type cleft. An example of the inconsistent width in the synaptic cleft of β2 KO mice is shown in Fig. 3. The synapses in the hippocampi of β2 KO mice also occasionally exhibit misapposition of membranes or even partial detachment of the pre- and post-synaptic membranes, leading to a seeming rupture of the synaptic cleft (Fig. 3)."

Fig.5: "Laminins provided by post-synaptic neurons stabilize neuronal synapses"
- "In control mixed cultures, where both GFP+ and GFP- neurons were from wild-type hippocampi (i.e., were from GFP+/β2+/+ and GFP-/β2+/+ animals), the synaptic density was not different between the two populations of dendrites (GFP+ dendrites had 99.5 ± 2.2% of the density of GFP- dendrites). These data demonstrate that the mere presence of GFP had no effect on the density of synapses. However, in mixed cultures where GFP+ dendrites were β2+/+ and GFP- dendrites were β2-/- (Fig. 5b), the lack of the laminin β2 chain from the post-synaptic neuron results in a 48% reduction in synaptic density on β2-/- dendrites (Fig. 5c), consistent with a post-synaptic source of the stabilizing laminins. ". Experimental description: - "For mixed cultures, an approximately equal number of hippocampal cells from wild-type or laminin β2-null mice..."
